patch.value: update-many --dry-run should allow filter-only preview for audit and bulk-selection workflows

During the 2026-07-06/07 all-status ecosystem audit, 'pm update-many --filter-status open --filter-metadata-missing --dry-run --limit 200 --json' failed because no mutation flags were provided. That is defensible for mutation execution, but dry-run is also the natural safe preview for bulk-selection filters. Duplicate check: searched all statuses for update-many dry-run no mutation flags filter-only audit; only closed pm-twtu covers the generic error message, while no open item tracks filter-only dry-run preview support.

update-many --dry-run can return the matched item set/count when only filters are provided, without requiring a dummy mutation flag; output is bounded and scriptable in json/toon; existing mutation-required behavior remains for non-dry-run execution; tests cover no-mutation dry-run, no-mutation real run, and at least one metadata filter.
Make bulk-selection filters safely inspectable before mutation
Improves audit workflows and reduces accidental bulk-mutation risk for agents.
Audits and bulk backfill planning need safe, exact previews before mutation; forcing a fake mutation flag is error-prone for agents.
